query: Moscow Oil Refinery Moskovsky NPZ Kapotnya drone attacks late June and early July 2026 - has it been targeted again after June 18 2026, current operational status, and frequency of Ukrainian drone strikes on Moscow refinery
answer:
## Research Complete

Here is a concise summary of my findings on the Moscow Oil Refinery (Moskovsky NPZ / Kapotnya):

**Has it been targeted again after June 18, 2026?** — Most likely **no confirmed fresh strike on the refinery itself**. The two firmly documented, cross-validated strikes are **June 16 and June 18**. Moscow city continued to face large raids afterward (notably June 25–26, when Russia claimed 660 drones intercepted), and one single-source Kyiv Post video claimed a June 25 re-strike on Kapotnya, but mainstream Reuters-sourced reporting treats the refinery as *already fully shut down from June 16/18*. After June 18, Ukrainian refinery strikes shifted elsewhere — **Slavyansk-EKO (June 29)** and **Lukoil Kstovo/Nizhny Novgorod (July 2)**.

**Current operational status** — **Completely offline.** Two refining units (incl. the Euro+/ELOU-AVT-6 primary unit) and multiple storage tanks destroyed; damage assessed >90%; ~53% of primary refining capacity hit. Reuters + satellite imagery: **won't resume before end-2026, possibly 2027**; repairs ≥6 months, hampered by sanctions. Wikipedia now states it closed "until 2027."

**Frequency of strikes** — Part of an intensifying campaign: ~30 successful refinery-region attacks in May 2026 alone; reportedly 8 of Russia's 10 largest refineries disabled; ~1/3 of Russian refining capacity (~2.14M bpd) offline; fuel rationing across 20–56+ regions.

**Note on uncertainty:** Sources conflict on whether a June 25 event was a genuine restrike or a re-narration of June 18 damage — I flagged this as unconfirmed. The full report, structured data points, and sources have been recorded via the StructuredOutput tool.
